elements.sent-2411-010: Exchange-driven growth (EDG) processes arise in the dynamics of cluster growth through the exchange of single units called monomers.
elements.sent-2411-011: It is usually assumed that clusters are completely identified by their mass or volume.
elements.sent-2411-012: The EDG model has applications in migration \cite{Ke1 , wealth exchange \cite{Isp , population dynamics \cite{Leyvraz and mean-field limit of a class of interacting particle systems \cite{Stef2, Lam ; see also \cite{Esenturk, Schlichting, barik2024discrete, Esenturk-L and the references therein.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-013: If $f_{j (t)$ denotes the density of clusters of size $j$ at time $t$, then the basic reaction for the exchange-driven growth phenomenon can be interpreted as \begin{equation}f_{j} (t)+ f_{k}(t)\;\;\xrightarrow{ {K_{j,k}}}{}\;\; f_{j-1}(t)+ f_{k+1}(t),\end{equation} for $t\ge 0$ and $j,k\in \mathbb{N _{0 := \mathbb{N}\cup\left\{0\right\}$ with $\mathbb{N :=\left\{1, 2, 3, \ldots\right\}$.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-014: Here, the interaction kernel $K_{j,k}$ depends only on the size of the clusters involved in the reaction.
elements.sent-2411-015: More precisely, in the phenomenon described above, the clusters of size $j$ export a monomer to the clusters of size $k$.
elements.sent-2411-016: Mathematically, the exchange-driven growth phenomenon is represented by an infinite system of non-linear ordinary differential equations \begin{equation} \dot{f}_{j}(t) = f_{j+1}(t)\sum_{k=0}^{\infty}K_{j+1,k} f_{k}(t)- f_{j}(t) \sum_{k=0}^{\infty}K_{j,k} f_{k}(t)\label{infode} \quad - f_{j}(t)\sum_{k=1}^{\infty}K_{k,j} f_{k}(t)+ f_{j-1}(t)\sum_{k=1}^{\infty }K_{k,j-1} f_{k}(t),\quad for\quad j\in N,\nonumber\end{equation} and \begin{equation}\quad \dot{ f}_{0}(t) = f_{1}(t)\sum_{k=0}^{\infty}K_{1,k} f_{k}(t)- f_{0}(t)\sum_{k=1}^{\infty }K_{k,0} f_{k}(t), \label{0-infode}\end{equation} with initial data \begin{equation}f_{j}(0) = f_{j,0}\quad for\quad j\in N_{0}.\label{infIC}\end{equation} The first and fourth terms in \eqref{infode represent the formation of clusters of size $j$ when either clusters of size $j+1$ release a monomer to other clusters, or clusters of size $j-1$ acquire a monomer from other clusters.}
elements.formula-2411-002: \begin{equation} \dot{f}_{j}(t) = f_{j+1}(t)\sum_{k=0}^{\infty}K_{j+1,k} f_{k}(t)- f_{j}(t) \sum_{k=0}^{\infty}K_{j,k} f_{k}(t)\label{infode} \quad - f_{j}(t)\sum_{k=1}^{\infty}K_{k,j} f_{k}(t)+ f_{j-1}(t)\sum_{k=1}^{\infty }K_{k,j-1} f_{k}(t),\quad for\quad j\in N,\nonumber\end{equation}
